---
title: "Chapter 16 - Daivasura-Sampad Vibhaga Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">दैवासुरसम्पद्विभागयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-15.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-17.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-16-1" data-reference="16.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-1">16.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Fearlessness, purity of heart, steadfastness in the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of knowledge,<br>
charity, self-restraint, sacrifice, study of scripture, austerity, and uprightness;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः ।<br>
दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-2" data-reference="16.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-2">16.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">nonviolence, truth, freedom from anger, renunciation, calm, freedom from slander, compassion for all beings, freedom from greed, gentleness, modesty, and steadiness;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्यागः शान्तिरपैशुनम् ।<br>
दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-3" data-reference="16.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-3">16.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">vigor, patience, fortitude, purity, freedom from malice and from pride—these belong to one born to a divine lot, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तेजः क्षमा धृतिः शौचमद्रोहो नातिमानिता ।<br>
भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-4" data-reference="16.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-4">16.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Hypocrisy, arrogance, conceit, anger, harshness, and ignorance belong, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, to one born to a demonic lot.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोधः पारुष्यमेव च ।<br>
अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-5" data-reference="16.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-5">16.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The divine lot leads to liberation, the demonic to bondage; do not grieve, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." aria-label="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." title="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." data-glossary-id="pandava">Pandava</span>—you are born to a divine lot.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दैवी सम्पद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता ।<br>
मा शुचः सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-6" data-reference="16.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-6">16.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">In this world there are two orders of created beings, the divine and the demonic; the divine has been described at length, now hear from me of the demonic, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">द्वौ भूतसर्गौ लोकेऽस्मिन्दैव आसुर एव च ।<br>
दैवो विस्तरशः प्रोक्त आसुरं पार्थ मे श‍ृणु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-7" data-reference="16.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-7">16.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The demonic do not know either action or its cessation; in them is found neither purity, nor right conduct, nor truth.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च जना न विदुरासुराः ।<br>
न शौचं नापि चाचारो न सत्यं तेषु विद्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-8" data-reference="16.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-8">16.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">They say the world is without truth, without ground, without a lord, arising from no orderly cause, brought about by desire alone.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">असत्यमप्रतिष्ठं ते जगदाहुरनीश्वरम् ।<br>
अपरस्परसम्भूतं किमन्यत्कामहैतुकम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-9" data-reference="16.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-9">16.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Holding to this view, these lost souls of feeble understanding and cruel deeds rise up as enemies for the ruin of the world.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एतां दृष्टिमवष्टभ्य नष्टात्मानोऽल्पबुद्धयः ।<br>
प्रभवन्त्युग्रकर्माणः क्षयाय जगतोऽहिताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-10" data-reference="16.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-10">16.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Clinging to insatiable desire, full of hypocrisy, pride, and arrogance, holding false notions through delusion, they act on impure resolves.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">काममाश्रित्य दुष्पूरं दम्भमानमदान्विताः ।<br>
मोहाद्गृहीत्वासद्ग्राहान्प्रवर्तन्तेऽशुचिव्रताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-11" data-reference="16.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-11">16.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Given over to measureless cares that end only with death, holding the satisfaction of desires to be the highest, certain that this is all,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">चिन्तामपरिमेयां च प्रलयान्तामुपाश्रिताः ।<br>
कामोपभोगपरमा एतावदिति निश्चिताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-12" data-reference="16.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-12">16.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">bound by a hundred cords of hope, devoted to desire and anger, they strive to amass hoards of wealth, by foul means, for the enjoyment of desires.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आशापाशशतैर्बद्धाः कामक्रोधपरायणाः ।<br>
ईहन्ते कामभोगार्थमन्यायेनार्थसञ्चयान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-13" data-reference="16.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-13">16.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">&quot;This I have gained today; this longing I shall now fulfill; this is mine, and this wealth too shall be mine;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम् ।<br>
इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-14" data-reference="16.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-14">16.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">that foe I have slain, and I shall slay the others too; I am the lord, I am the enjoyer, I am successful, strong, and happy;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">असौ मया हतः शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि ।<br>
ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-15" data-reference="16.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-15">16.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am rich and high-born; who else is equal to me? I will sacrifice, I will give, I will rejoice&quot;—so they say, deluded by ignorance.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया ।<br>
यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-16" data-reference="16.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-16">16.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Led astray by many fancies, caught in the net of delusion, addicted to the enjoyment of desires, they fall into a foul hell.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनेकचित्तविभ्रान्ता मोहजालसमावृताः ।<br>
प्रसक्ताः कामभोगेषु पतन्ति नरकेऽशुचौ ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-17" data-reference="16.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-17">16.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Self-important, stubborn, filled with the pride and intoxication of wealth, they offer sacrifices in name only, with show and contrary to the rules.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आत्मसम्भाविताः स्तब्धा धनमानमदान्विताः ।<br>
यजन्ते नामयज्ञैस्ते दम्भेनाविधिपूर्वकम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-18" data-reference="16.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-18">16.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Clinging to ego, force, insolence, desire, and anger, these spiteful people hate me in their own and others&#x27; bodies.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं च संश्रिताः ।<br>
मामात्मपरदेहेषु प्रद्विषन्तोऽभ्यसूयकाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-19" data-reference="16.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-19">16.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">These haters, cruel and base among men, the worst of the world, I hurl again and again into demonic wombs, into the rounds of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="samsara: The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment." aria-label="samsara: The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment." title="samsara: The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment." data-glossary-id="samsara">samsara</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तानहं द्विषतः क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान् ।<br>
क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-20" data-reference="16.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-20">16.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Cast into demonic wombs, birth after birth, the deluded, never reaching me, O son of Kunti, sink from there to the lowest state.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आसुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि ।<br>
मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-21" data-reference="16.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-21">16.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Threefold is this gate of hell, ruin of the self: desire, anger, and greed; therefore one should give up these three.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः ।<br>
कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-22" data-reference="16.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-22">16.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Freed from these three gates of darkness, O son of Kunti, a person works for his own good and so reaches the supreme goal.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एतैर्विमुक्तः कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नरः ।<br>
आचरत्यात्मनः श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-23" data-reference="16.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-23">16.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But he who casts aside the rule of scripture and acts on the urge of desire reaches neither perfection, nor happiness, nor the supreme goal.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यः शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्तते कामकारतः ।<br>
न स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-16-24" data-reference="16.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-16-24">16.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Therefore let scripture be your measure in deciding what should and should not be done; knowing what the rule of scripture declares, you should act here in the world.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तस्माच्छास्त्रं प्रमाणं ते कार्याकार्यव्यवस्थितौ ।<br>
ज्ञात्वा शास्त्रविधानोक्तं कर्म कर्तुमिहार्हसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
